_comment: Masterman Assessment v2 — 20 approved questions. 4 modules × (4 Likert + 1 Scenario). Do NOT edit question IDs, axis, module, type, weight, or scoringDirection without updating lib/scoring.ts. Text edits only need JSON changes.

questions.text: I know exactly why Allah put me on this earth, and I can say it in one sentence.
Most weeks, I feel like I am reacting to life instead of leading it.
When my faith is tested, I return to the Qur'an and Sunnah before I return to my own thinking.
I often feel I am playing a role I did not choose.
Your boss or business partner asks you to do something that crosses an Islamic line. Nothing illegal — just wrong. What do you actually do?

options.label: Push back respectfully and refuse, even if it costs me.
Do it this once and tell myself I'll fix it later.
Find a workaround so I don't have to choose.
Refuse and explain my reasoning openly.

questions.text: When I feel anger rising, I can stop myself before I speak.
I keep promises I make to myself, even small ones.
My desires control more of my day than I control of them.
I do muhasaba (self-accounting) on most days.
It is 11pm. You are tired. You told yourself you would pray Isha and sleep. Your phone is in your hand. What actually happens most nights?

options.label: I put the phone down, pray, and sleep.
I scroll for 10 minutes, then pray and sleep.
I scroll, lose track, and pray late and rushed.
I scroll until I fall asleep and miss Isha.

questions.text: My wife knows I will lead the household even when it is hard.
questions.textHypothetical: When things get hard, I take the lead instead of waiting for someone else to step up.

questions.text: I avoid hard conversations with my wife and hope they pass.
questions.textHypothetical: I tend to avoid hard conversations and hope they pass on their own.

questions.text: My children see me pray more often than they see me on my phone.
questions.textHypothetical: The people closest to me see me pray more often than they see me on my phone.

questions.text: When something is wrong at home, I usually wait for my wife to bring it up.
questions.textHypothetical: When something feels off, I usually wait for others to bring it up first.

questions.text: Your wife tells you she is unhappy with how things are at home. You do not fully agree. What do you do?

options.label: Listen fully, ask questions, then lead a real conversation.
Listen, get defensive, and try to explain my side.
Agree to keep the peace and change nothing.
Listen, take a day to reflect, and come back with a plan.

questions.text: I have at least two Muslim brothers I can call when I am struggling.
Most weeks I go without real conversation with another Muslim man.
There is at least one brother who would correct me if I was wrong, and I would listen.
I tell myself I do not need other men — I just need Allah.
You are going through the hardest month of your life. Who actually knows about it?

options.label: Two or three brothers I trust — and we have talked about it.
One brother knows part of it.
Nobody. I am handling it alone.
